name: Biweekly company shared across non-canary payroll specs
description: Single onboarded company shared by all read-only / non-mutating payroll lifecycle specs (regular-payroll, blockers-view-all, off-cycle, transition, payroll-review-existing, payroll-cancel-alert, payroll-breadcrumb-back). All of those specs only navigate to /?flow=payroll or /?flow=transition and assert tabs/landing/blocker surfaces — none reads decoration-specific keys (employeeIds.alice etc.) — so the base demo's seed roster is sufficient and we only overlay a biweekly pay schedule (transition.spec.ts has the fixture inject scenario.paySchedule.uuid into the URL). The canary suite intentionally uses its own scenario id because it mutates state across tests; lifecycle specs share this one to collapse what was previously 4 separate provisioned companies into 1.
